title: Vendor Transition from 120 Water to Trupanti, Community Organization Opposition, and Filter Type Debate
summary: Discussion of 120 Water to Trupanti vendor transition: 120 Water was emergency hire recommended by Buffalo, community organizations strongly opposed them and city was overcharged. Trupanti won RFP after reference checks (serves St. Paul, Minneapolis, most of Texas); first NY State client, using same lab as 120 Water. Contract awaiting law department approval (council already voted). Community orgs not consulted on Trupanti selection. Lead Safe CNY Coalition engagement: commissioner attends monthly meetings, speaks on updates, works with 'Latoya' contact. Filter debate intensifies: 6,000+ Brita pitchers distributed in 2024 to all K-grade-1 students across city and charter schools; most filters reportedly discarded (too large, taste complaints). Council member challenges pitcher-only policy -- EPA requires certified lead-reduction filter, not specifically Brita pitcher; city should explore faucet-mount alternatives. Commissioner cites EPA standard requirements. Chair Majok pushes for consistent education through community centers, libraries, TNT organizations, and bold-letter mailings. Transportation barriers identified -- commissioner personally delivers filters to residents unable to travel. Trupanti contract includes provision for additional voluntary two-family home testing beyond EPA-mandated 100-home tier-one pool.

political_signal.description: Council questioning 120 Water vendor selection history, community org exclusion from procurement, and challenging pitcher-only filter policy as potentially wasteful

processing_notes: Law Department in detected_departments is false positive from 'contract to go through law' discussion; excluded
'Latoya' = Lead Safe CNY Coalition contact; not in entity registry
120 Water = prior vendor (recommended by Buffalo, used for 2 semesters); Trupanti = new vendor via RFP
EPA filter requirement debate: city says Brita is mandated, council member argues only lead-reduction certification is required
Segment 3 of 7 from Public Works and Infrastructure Committee Meeting, January 20th 2026
Potential duplicate detected: overlaps with CTX-COUNCIL-2026-00052 -- linked, not skipped
2026-03-21: Linked to CLUSTER-LEAD-REMEDIATION-2026Q1 (7 records from Jan 20 Public Works Committee lead water discussion).
